normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.7 - Telecommunications Facilities and Communications Equipment
scope_summary: Provides location, siting and design criteria for telecommunications facilities and communications equipment in the City of Melville that are not exempt low-impact facilities under the Telecommunications Act 1997. Preferred locations are existing telecommunications sites (co-location), then specified non-residential zones or MRS-reserved land; there is a presumption against facilities in conservation/foreshore areas including Wireless Hill Park. Also sets standards for residential communications equipment (TV aerials permitted as of right; antennae/dishes not visible from the street; otherwise assessed against R-Codes Design Principles), decommissioning, application documentation per SPP 5.2 cl 5.3, and advertising/notification requirements.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: communications equipment on non-residential properties not meeting clauses 4-5

key_numeric_standards.topic: communications equipment height adjoining residential property
key_numeric_standards.unit: m max above ground level (or screened from view)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: equipment on non-residential property adjoining a residential property

key_numeric_standards.topic: sensitive-use proximity trigger for design scrutiny
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: new telecommunications infrastructure near residential properties, schools, child day care
